questions.question: What does congruence mean in geometry?
questions.correct_answer: Figures that have exactly the same shape and the same size
questions.explanation: If two figures are congruent, they can be superimposed to cover each other exactly.

questions.question: What is the symbol used to denote congruence?
questions.explanation: This symbol combines '=' (equal size) and '~' (same shape).

questions.question: What is meant by 'correspondence' in congruent triangles?
questions.correct_answer: The matching of vertices, sides, and angles between two triangles
questions.explanation: In $\triangle ABC \cong \triangle PQR$, $A$ corresponds to $P$, $B$ to $Q$, and $C$ to $R$.

questions.question: What is the SSS Congruence Criterion?
questions.correct_answer: Side-Side-Side: Three sides of one triangle are equal to three sides of another
questions.explanation: If all three pairs of corresponding sides are equal, the triangles are congruent.

questions.question: What does the SAS criterion require regarding the angle?
questions.correct_answer: The angle must be the 'included angle' between the two equal sides
questions.explanation: Side-Angle-Side only works if the angle is located between the two known sides.

questions.question: Define the ASA Congruence Criterion.
questions.correct_answer: Angle-Side-Angle: Two angles and the included side are equal
questions.explanation: The side must be the one that connects the two equal angles.

questions.question: When is the RHS Congruence Criterion used?
questions.explanation: It requires a Right angle, equal Hypotenuse, and one equal Side.

questions.question: What does the abbreviation CPCT stand for?
questions.explanation: Once triangles are proved congruent, their remaining matching parts are automatically equal.

questions.question: Are two squares with the same side length congruent?
questions.explanation: Since all angles are $90^{\circ}$ and sides are equal, they will match perfectly.

questions.question: Why is congruence important in manufacturing?
questions.correct_answer: It ensures that mass-produced parts are identical and interchangeable
questions.explanation: Parts like car engine components must be congruent to fit and function correctly.

notes.title: Superimposition
notes.content: The method of placing one figure over another to check if they match exactly.

notes.title: Congruence of Circles
notes.content: Two circles are congruent if they have the same radius length.

notes.title: Included Angle vs. Included Side
notes.content: SAS requires the included angle; ASA requires the included side.

notes.title: RHS Necessity
notes.content: In right triangles, the hypotenuse is the side opposite the $90^{\circ}$ angle.

notes.title: Order of Naming
notes.content: Always name congruent triangles in the correct order of their corresponding vertices.

facts.fact: Two line segments are congruent if they have the same length.
SSA (Side-Side-Angle) is NOT a valid congruence criterion.
If two triangles are congruent, their areas are always equal.
Congruence is used in architecture to create perfectly symmetrical structures.
